PROCLAMATION
To the General Assembly
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Whereas, the people of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky have indicated in no uncertain terms their
desire to have an opportunity to select the nominees
for state offices in state -wide primaries ; and
Whereas , the right of the people to have pri-
maries is a fundamental one and ought not to be
denied .
Therefore , in order to insure to the people the
restoration of their right to select the party nomi-
nees for all state offices at primaries , and in accord-
ance with the power vested in me by the Constitu-
tion of the Commonwealth of Kentucky to convene
the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky in extraordinary session , I , as Governor
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , do issue this ,
my proclamation, convening the General Assembly
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky in extraordinary
session at the seat of government at Frankfort ,
Kentucky , at 12 o'clock , noon , on Friday , February
8 , 1935 , for the sole purpose of considering the fol-
lowing subject :
(1 ) To enact a compulsory primary law for the
selection of all party nominees for all state offices
at state -wide primaries .
You will therefore , in accordance with this proc-
lamation assemble in said extraordinary session at
the seat of government at Frankfort , Kentucky , on
the day and date above written , to consider the sub-
ject herein mentioned , and only said subject .
Given under my hand at 10:30 o'clock , a . m ., this
the 6th day of February , 1935 .
By the Governor :
( Signed ) A. B. CHANDLER ,
Governor of Kentucky .
SARA W. MAHAN , Secretary of State.
By : C. W. WILSON , Asst . Secretary of State .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
OF THE
COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
PASSED AT THE EXTRAORDINARY SESSION OF THE GEN-
ERAL ASSEMBLY , WHICH WAS BEGUN IN THE CITY OF
FRANKFORT , KENTUCKY , ON FRIDAY , FEBRUARY
EIGHTH , 1935 , AND ENDED ON WEDNESDAY , FEBRUARY
THE TWENTY -SEVENTH , 1935 .
CHAPTER 1
AN ACT relating to primary elections , and repealing Section
1549a -1, Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's 1930 Edition , and amend-
ing Sections 1549a -2, 1549a -3 , 1549a -4 , and 1550-1 , Kentucky
Statutes , Carroll's 1930 Edition , and declaring an emergency .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 1549a - 1 , Kentucky Statutes ,
Carroll's 1930 Edition , be and the same hereby is
repealed .
§. 2. That Section 1549a -2, Kentucky Statutes ,
Carroll's 1930 Edition , be and the same hereby is
amended by striking said section in its entirety and
reenacting said section to read as follows , to -wit :
Each and every political party shall nominate its
candidates for the offices of governor , lieutenant
governor , secretary of state , attorney general ,
auditor of public accounts , state treasurer , commis-
sioner of agriculture , labor and statistics , superin-
tendent of public instruction , clerk of the court of
appeals , to be voted for at the next succeeding gen-
eral election , at the primary elections to be held at
the time and in the manner provided by law . No
governing authority of any political party shall
have the power to nominate any candidate for either

6 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
of the aforesaid offices or to provide any other
means or method of nominating candidates for
either of said offices than by primary elections as
hereinabove provided .
$ 3. Section 1549a -3, Kentucky Statutes , Car-
roll's 1930 Edition , be and the same hereby is
amended by striking said section in its entirety and
reenacting said section to read as follows , to -wit :
Any qualified elector who is a member of a political
party within the meaning of this Act , and who has
affiliated with and supported the nominees of the
party at whose hands he seeks the nomination , shall
have his name printed on the official ballot of his
party for an office to which he is eligible in any pri-
mary election held under the provisions of this act
and/or the provisions of the general primary elec-
tion laws of this State upon filing with the proper
officer at the proper time a notification and declara-
tion and in the manner and form now prescribed by
law .
The Secretary of State shall have no power or
authority to print or cause or permit to be printed
upon the ballots for any general election the name
or names of any candidate or candidates as the
nominee or nominees of any political party or
parties , as the term " political party " is defined in
Section 1550-5 , Carroll's Ky . Statutes , 1930 Edi-
tion , or the name or names of any candidate or can-
didates under the emblem of any political party or
parties for any of the offices hereinabove referred
to, except the names of such candidates as shall have
been duly and regularly nominated at a primary
election held as provided in this act .
§ 4. That Section 1549a -4, Kentucky Statutes ,
Carroll's 1930 Edition , be and the same hereby is
amended by striking said section in its entirety and
reenacting it to read as follows , to-wit : Nothing in

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 7
this act shall be construed to prevent any political
organization or party , not constituting a political
party within the definition embraced in Section
1550-5 , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's 1930 Edition ,
from making their nominations for any of the of-
fices mentioned in this act as they are now per-
mitted by law to make same .
§ 5. That Section 1550-1 , Kentucky Statutes ,
Carroll's 1930 Edition , be and the same hereby is
amended by striking said section in its entirety and
reenacting it to read as follows , to -wit : Hereafter
all candidates for elective offices to be voted for at
any general election shall be nominated by a pri-
mary held in accordance with the provisions of
this act . Provided that if a vacancy occurs after
any such nomination , by death or otherwise , the
governing authority of such party may provide for
filling such vacancy or vacancies and the making of
such nominations , and when such nominations have
been so made the certificates of nomination shall be
signed by the chairman and secretary of the gov-
erning authority of the party making same and
shall be filed in the same manner as certificates of
nomination at a primary election . The provisions
of this Act shall not apply to candidates for trus-
tees of common schools or members of school boards
nor to trustees in towns of the fifth and sixth classes
nor to candidates for presidential electors , but such
candidates for such offices shall be nominated and
have their nominations certified as now, or may be
hereafter provided by law .
5a . In the event no candidate shall receive a
majority of all the votes cast for the candidates of
his party for the office which he seeks , at the Pri-
mary Election held as provided by Section 1550-3 ,
Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's 1930 Edition , then a
second Primary Election shall be held on the first

8 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
Saturday in September following , between the
hours of Six A. M. and Four P. M. , in each of the
voting precincts in the State , for the purpose of
determining the choice of the political party be-
tween the two candidates for each of the offices re-
ferred to in the first section of this Act , which
receives the highest number of votes at the August
Primary Election next preceeding said run -off Pri-
mary Election ;
Provided , in the event any candidate in the pre-
liminary Primary Election shall receive a ma-
jority of all the votes cast by his party in said pre-
liminary Primary for candidates for the office which
he seeks, then in that event , such candidate shall be
deemed the nominee of such party for the office
which he seeks , without entering the Second Pri-
mary Election herein provided for.
The Second Primary Election herein provided
for shall be conducted in the same manner as the
primary election held on the first Saturday in
August , as provided for by the Statutes , except no
names shall be printed on the ballots in said second
Primary Election except the two candidates receiv-
ing the highest number of votes cast by the political
party for the office which they seek at the prelim-
inary Primary Election , and the Secretary of State
shall , not less than ten days prior to the first Satur-
day in September , certify to their respective County
Clerks of the State , the names of the two candidates
for each office , whose names shall be printed upon
the ballot to be used in said second Primary Elec-
tion to be held on the First Saturday in September ,
as herein provided for , and the same general provi-
sions of law in all respects shall apply to the second
Primary Election provided for herein , as now ap-
plies to the conduct of the preliminary Primary
Election .

1
ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 9
§ 6. WHEREAS , one of the major political parties
through its duly authorized governing authority has
called a State Convention to meet in Lexington ,
Kentucky , on May 14 , 1935 , to nominate candidates
for Governor , Lieutenant Governor , Secretary of
State , Attorney General , Auditor of Public Ac-
counts , State Treasurer , Commissioner of Agricul-
ture , Labor and Statistics , Superintendent of Pub-
lic Instruction , and Clerk of the Court of Appeals ,
now , therefore , an emergency is hereby declared to
exist , and this Act shall become effective immedi-
ately upon its passage and approval by the Gov-
ernor .
§ 7. All acts and parts of acts in conflict here-
with , to the extent of such conflict , are hereby re-
pealed .
§ 8. This bill shall be known and may be cited
as the " Gaines - Myers Compulsory Primary Law. '
A. B. CHANDLER ,
President of Senate .
W. E. ROGERS ,
Speaker of the House of Representatives .
RUBY LAFFOON ,
Governor of Kentucky .
Approved February 27 , 1935 .
CHAPTER 2
AN ACT providing that members of the House and Senate and
the regular employees of the House and Senate receive per
diem , mileage and extras from and including February eight ,
One thousand nine hundred thirty -five , for the February One
thousand nine hundred thirty -five Special Session and appro-
priating Fifty Thousand (Fifty Thousand Dollars ) Dollars for
that purpose .
Whereas , a Special Session of the Legislature was called for
Friday , February eight , One thousand nine hundred thirty.
five ; and

10 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
Whereas , the members of the House of Representatives , of
Kentucky , the members of the Senate of Kentucky , the em
ployees of the House of Representatives and the employees
of the Senate , are entitled to their pay , starting with the
said eighth day of February , One thousand nine hundred
thirty -five , and including the same ; and
Whereas , due to litigation which went into the Court of
Appeals of the Commonwealth , no member and no employee
of the House of Representatives or the Senate could be cer-
tain of their pay before the decision of the Court of Appeals
which was handed down on the thirteenth day of February ,
One thousand nine hundred thirty -five ; and
Whereas , there has been no appropriation made for the payment
of necessary expenses to conduct the present extraordinary
session ; now therefore ,
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That each and every member of the House of
Representatives and each and every member of the
Senate , each and every employee of the House of
Representatives and each and every employee of
the Senate , according to the official lists adopted by
the said House of Representatives and the said
Senate for their regular employees be , and they
hereby are , entitled to receive their pay from and
as of the eighth day of February , One thousand
nine hundred thirty -five , and including the eighth
day of February , One thousand nine hundred thirty-
five , and they, and each of them , are entitled to re-
ceive their pay, per diem , mileage , and extras , ac-
cording to the law in such case , made and provided ,
and the Auditor of Public Accounts of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky is hereby ordered and com-
manded to issue his warrant on the State Treas-
urer , to the said Representatives and Senators and
employees of the said House and Senate for all
sums due them from and after February eighth ,
One thousand nine hundred thirty -five , and that
the Auditor of Public Accounts of the Common-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 11
wealth of Kentucky be directed to issue warrants on
the State Treasurer for the said sums and that the
Treasurer be ordered to disburse the same from and
including the eighth day of February , One thousand
nine hundred thirty -five , up until the last day that
the Extraordinary Session remains in session in
Frankfort ; and all officers of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky are hereby commanded and directed to
obey this Act and hereof to fail not . For the pur-
poses of paying the expenses of the General Assem-
bly and its help , there is hereby appropriated from
the General funds of the Commonwealth , the sum of
Fifty Thousand (Fifty Thousand Dollars ) Dollars .
W. E. ROGERS ,
Speaker of the House of Representatives .
A. B. CHANDLER ,
President of the Senate .
RUBY LAFFOON ,
Governor of the State of Kentucky .
Approved February 27th, 1935 .
CHAPTER 3
CONCURRENT RESOLUTION , requesting that the Congress of
the United States , without further delay pass the Frazier-
Lemke Farm Refinance Bill , S. 212 and H. R. 2066 .
Whereas , unless immediate relief is given , hundreds and thou-
sands of additional farmers will lose their farms and their
homes , and millions more will be forced into our cities and
villages, and the army of the unemployed will necessarily in-
crease to alarming proportions ; and
Whereas , there is no adequate way of definancing existing agri-
cultural indebtedness , and the farmers are at the mercy of
their mortgagees and creditors throughout this state and
nation ; and
Whereas , the Frazier -Lemke Refinance bill , being S. 212 and
H. R. 2066 , in the Congress of the United States , provides for
the liquidating and refinancing of agricultural indebtedness

12 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
at a reduced rate of interest, thru the Farm Credit Admin-
istration and Federal Land Banks ; and
Whereas , the Frazier -Lemke bill has the endorsement of
twenty-two state legislatures , and in addition the lower
Houses of the state of New York and Delaware , and of many
Commercial Clubs , Chambers of Commerce , bank organiza-
tions , and of business and professional men and women , as
well as the great majority of the farmers of this nation ; and
Whereas , the enactment of this bill will have a vital effect not
only upon agriculture , but upon all classes of industry ; and
Whereas , agriculture is the basic industry of this country , and
there can be no recovery until agriculture is put upon a sound
basis :
Now , Therefore , Be it Resolved , That it is the
sense of your memorialists the members of the Ken-
tucky Legislative Assembly of the State of Ken-
tucky, the Senate and the House concurring , that
the Congress of the United States should enact the
Frazier -Lemke bill without further delay .
Be it Further Resolved , That a copy of this Me-
morial , duly authenticated , be sent by the Secretary
of State to the President of the Senate and Speaker
of the House of Representatives of the United
States , and to each Senator and Representative in
Congress from this State , to the President of the
United States , and to U. S. Senator Lynn J. Frazier
and Congressman William Lemke .
A. B. CHANDLER ,
President of the Senate .
W. E. ROGERS ,
Speaker of the House of Representatives .
RUBY LAFFOON ,
Governor of Kentucky .
Approved this February 27 , 1935 .
CHAPTER 4
RESOLUTION providing a contingent Fund for the clerk of
'the Senate and the clerk of the House .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 13
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That for the purpose of paying such expenses as
the Chief Clerk of the Senate of Kentucky is di-
rected to incur by the Senate of Kentucky , there is
hereby appropriated out of the General Revenues
of the State , as a contingent fund , the sum of Two
Hundred Fifty ( Two Hundred Fifty Dollars ) Dol-
lars , and for the purpose of paying the expenses in-
curred by the Chief Clerk of the House of Represen-
tatives of the Commonwealth , under the direction
of said House there is hereby appropriated out of
the General Revenues of the State , as a contingent
fund , the sum of Two Hundred Fifty ( Two Hundred
Fifty Dollars ) Dollars . Said respective funds shall
be paid out on warrant of the Auditor , issued on
requisition of the Chief Clerk of the Senate and of
the Chief Clerk of the House of Representatives .
Whereas, expenses were incurred by the Senate
and by the House of Representatives at the last
Special Session of the Legislature , which has never
been paid , it is directed that the expenses incurred
by the Senate and by the House of Representatives
at the last Special Session in One thousand nine
hundred thirty-four , be paid out of the contingent
fund hereby created .
Whereas , it is necessary that the said Clerks
shall have immediate use of said contingent funds ,
therefore an emergency is hereby declared to exist ,
and this resolution shall take effect from and after
its passage and approval by the Governor .
W. E. ROGERS ,
Speaker of the House of Representatives
A. B. CHANDLER ,
President of the Senate .
RUBY LAFFOON ,
Governor of Kentucky .
Approved February 27, 1935 .

14 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
CHAPTER 5
A JOINT and concurrent Resolution providing for the payment
of money to Raymond Bowman and George Webb , for extra
services in caring for the offices and corridors of the House
and Senate chambers , during the Special Session of the Legis-
lature of One thousand nine hundred thirty -five .
Whereas , the aforesaid persons , employees of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , have been required to perform overtime
and extra services because of the Special Session of the One
thousand nine hundred thirty -five General Assembly ; now ,
therefore ,
Be it Resolved by the House of Representatives
of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky , the Senate of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky concurring therein :
That Raymond Bowman and George Webb are
each hereby allowed the sum of one dollar ( one dol-
lar ) per day for each day of the Special One thou-
sand nine hundred thirty -five session of the Gen-
eral Assembly, which allowance shall be in addition
to the salaries they now receive for their services .
The appropriation hereby made is to be paid out
of any funds in the Treasury not otherwise appro-
priated and the Auditor of Public Accounts is here-
by ordered and directed to draw his warrant upon
the Treasurer to each of the above named persons
for the amount due them as herein provided .
W. E. ROGERS ,
Speaker of the House of Representatives .
A. B. CHANDLER ,
President of the Senate .
RUBY LAFFOON ,
Governor of Kentucky .
Approved February 28 , 1935 .
CHAPTER 6
WHEREAS , the Honorable Allie W. Young, the Honorable
James Breathitt , the Honorable A. L. Hill , the Honorable

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 15
Avery Sartin , and the Honorable Lafe Spencer , were duly
elected and qualified members of the General Assembly of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky for the full terms of the
General Assembly beginning in January , One thousand nine
hundred thirty -four , for a period of two years , and
Whereas , these members have been unable to attend the ses
sions because of illness and death , and
Whereas , this being an extraordinary session of the General
Assembly during the terms of the respective gentlemen as
set out , and
Whereas , the family of these members would have received re-
muneration benefits had they been able to attend ; now , there-
fore,
Be it Resolved by the House of Representatives
of Kentucky , the Senate concurring therein :
That the wives of the said deceased members ,
namely the Honorable Allie W. Young , the Honor-
able James Breathitt , the Honorable A. L. Hill , the
Honorable Avery Sartin , and the Honorable Lafe
Spencer , be paid their per diem of ten dollars ( ten
dollars ) as members of the Senate and House of
Representatives for the entire special session of one
thousand nine hundred thirty -five as though the
members had been personally present at each of
said sessions during said special session , and
That the Clerk of the Senate and the Clerk of the
House of Representatives are hereby directed to
issue their respective orders on the Auditor of Pub-
lic Accounts of the Commonwealth of Kentucky for
the said sums that will be due when the total number
of legislative days of the special session shall have
been determined , the said orders to be payable to
the widows of the said deceased members , and the
Auditor of Public Accounts shall issue his warrants

16 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
on the Treasurer of the Commonwealth for the
amounts thereof .
W. E. ROGERS ,
Speaker of the House of Representatives .
A. B. CHANDLER ,
President of the Senate .
RUBY LAFFOON ,
Governor of Kentucky .
Approved the 28th day of February , 1935 .
CHAPTER 7
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That when the House of Representatives and the
Senate adjourn on this the twenty- seventh day of
February , One thousand nine hundred thirty -five ,
that they do adjourn sine die .
W. E. ROGERS ,
Speaker of the House of Representatives .
A. B. CHANDLER ,
President of the Senate .
RUBY LAFFOON ,
Governor of Kentucky .
Approved the 28th day of February , 1935 .
